Title: A small peptide APP3-14 disrupts pathogen–insect mutualism by modulating plant MYC2-mediated olfactory defense

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed transiently and stably in citrus; acts during CLas infection
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Volatile repellents involved in olfactory defense against psyllids
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppresses terpene biosynthesis, increases psyllid attraction and fitness, and promotes HLB pathogen infection
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient and stable expression in citrus; lysozyme inhibition assay; mutant analysis; yeast two-hybrid; luciferase assay; dimerization/competition assays; RT-qPCR; GC–MS; psyllid choice and survival assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among tested CLas SDEs, SDE5 uniquely increased citrus attractiveness to psyllids. 2. SDE5 acts as a bacterial C-type lysozyme inhibitor, and its lysozyme-inhibitory activity is required for suppression of TPS21-7 and psyllid attraction. 3. SDE5 suppresses MYC2-mediated terpene defense by promoting PUB10/PUB21-dependent MYC2 degradation and by disrupting MYC2 dimerization.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhances pathogen infection and psyllid fitness; suppresses plant defense genes
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: NCBI sequence accession reported; no omics dataset accession provided
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter, constitutive, for transient assays; sTRV system for citrus expression
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Effector secreted by CLas during infection
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ser68 is critical for lysozyme inhibitor activity; mutant S68A lost inhibitory and psyllid-attraction effects but still interacted with MYC2
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value anti-virulence target; inhibition of SDE5 or blocking its interaction with MYC2/PUB ligases could restore terpene defense and reduce vector attraction

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Repel psyllid vectors and support olfactory defense
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances volatile terpenoid emission and reduces psyllid attraction to HLB-positive citrus by stabilizing MYC2 and diminishing SDE5 function
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Peptide treatment of infected citrus; psyllid two-choice assay; lysozyme inhibition interference assay; GC–MS terpene analysis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. APP3-14 treatment reduced psyllid attraction to HLB-positive citrus compared with control peptide treatment. 2. APP3-14 reduced the lysozyme inhibitor efficacy of SDE5. 3. APP3-14 increased emission of β-myrcene and β-caryophyllene, consistent with restoration of MYC2-regulated terpene defense.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Described as a human microbiota-derived anti-proteolysis peptide with bactericidal activity and MYC2-stabilizing function
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising field-deployable treatment to simultaneously suppress HLB pathogen function and repel psyllid vectors

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly repressed in CLas-infected citrus and in SDE5-expressing or MYC2-silenced plants; activated by MYC2
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Volatile repellents of the Asian citrus psyllid; contribute to olfactory anti-herbivore defense
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes/converts (E,E)-farnesyl diphosphate into β-caryophyllene as the major product and minor sesquiterpenes including (E)-β-farnesene
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf volatile emissions from citrus branches/leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes β-caryophyllene and (E)-β-farnesene production and reduces psyllid attraction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; recombinant protein enzyme assay in E. coli; GC–MS product identification; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; transient expression in citrus; psyllid Y-tube and two-choice assays
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: CLas infection; transient SDE5 expression; MYC2 silencing/overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TPS21-7 was the most strongly repressed TPS21 homolog in CLas-infected citrus. 2. Recombinant TPS21-7 used (E,E)-farnesyl diphosphate to produce β-caryophyllene as the major sesquiterpene and (E)-β-farnesene as a minor product. 3. Expression of TPS21-7 in citrus reduced psyllid attraction, linking this enzyme to terpene-based olfactory defense.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The gene was identified as a putative Arabidopsis TPS21 homolog; promoter contains MYC2-bound G-box-like motifs
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering citrus with enhanced volatile repellents against psyllids; could be stacked with MYC2 stabilization strategies such as APP3-14 or reduced PUB21 activity

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Silenced or overexpressed experimentally; endogenous protein strongly reduced in CLas-infected citrus
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Repel psyllid vectors and contribute to anti-herbivore olfactory defense
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates TPS21 genes, especially TPS21-7, by binding G-box-like promoter elements
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key positive regulator of citrus terpene-based olfactory defense against psyllids
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes volatile terpene emission, reduces psyllid attraction, and increases psyllid mortality
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS silencing; transient and stable overexpression; RT-qPCR; GC–MS; EMSA; luciferase transactivation assay; psyllid choice assays; survival assay
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms MYC2 homodimers; SDE5 disrupts this dimerization
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonate signaling; CLas effector SDE5; PUB10/PUB21-mediated ubiquitination
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: CLas infection; psyllid feeding context; SDE5 expression; APP3-14 treatment
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYC2 positively regulates citrus defense against psyllids by promoting terpene synthesis. 2. MYC2 directly binds G-box-like motifs in the TPS21-7 promoter and activates its transcription. 3. Silencing MYC2 reduced TPS21 expression and β-caryophyllene emission, whereas overexpression reduced psyllid attraction and increased psyllid mortality.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: NCBI sequence accession reported; no omics dataset accession provided
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter, constitutive, for transient expression constructs
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A prime target for engineering HLB/psyllid resistance via stabilization or overexpression; compatible with APP3-14-based protection and reducing PUB10/PUB21 activity
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Besides volatile defense, MYC2 also enhances non-volatile defense factors affecting psyllid survival

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Analyzed by stable overexpression and dominant-negative overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Protein stability control of MYC2, thereby terpene biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Negatively affects TPS21 genes indirectly by promoting MYC2 degradation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Negative controller of terpene-based olfactory defense through MYC2 destabilization
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces terpene emission and increases psyllid attraction when overexpressed; dominant-negative PUB21DN has opposite effect
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein interaction/ubiquitination assay; transient co-expression degradation assay; stable overexpression and dominant-negative lines; RT-qPCR; GC–MS; psyllid choice assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PUB21 ubiquitinates MYC2 and promotes its degradation. 2. PUB21 overexpression reduced TPS21 expression and lowered β-caryophyllene and (E)-β-farnesene emission. 3. PUB21 overexpression increased psyllid attraction, while dominant-negative PUB21DN increased terpenes and reduced attraction.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: NCBI sequence accession reported; no omics dataset accession provided
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for gene editing or dominant-negative manipulation to stabilize MYC2 and improve terpene-based psyllid resistance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SDE5 does not change PUB21 E3 activity itself but enhances PUB21-mediated ubiquitination of MYC2

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Analyzed by transient expression and in degradation/ubiquitination assays
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Protein stability control of MYC2, thereby terpene biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Negatively affects terpene biosynthesis indirectly through MYC2 degradation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Negative regulator contributing to suppression of MYC2-dependent olfactory defense
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes MYC2 degradation and increases psyllid attraction when transiently overexpressed
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein interaction/ubiquitination assay; transient co-expression degradation assay; psyllid attraction assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Citrus PUB10 interacts with and ubiquitinates MYC2. 2. PUB10 promotes MYC2 degradation, and SDE5 further enhances this effect. 3. Transient overexpression of PUB10 increased psyllid attraction, supporting a negative role in terpene-mediated defense.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: NCBI sequence accession reported; no omics dataset accession provided
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential editing target to stabilize MYC2 and strengthen terpene-based vector resistance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PUB10DN mutant largely lost MYC2 ubiquitination activity
